prompt: Create a photorealistic square-format close-up portrait of a 78-year-old Icelandic fisherman standing at a stormy harbor. The man fills most of the frame from chest up, positioned slightly left of center, with deeply weathered sunburned skin, heavy forehead wrinkles, blue eyes, short gray hair, salt-and-pepper stubble, and flecks of sea salt or snow on his cheeks, eyebrows, beard, and dark navy cable-knit wool sweater. He gazes pensively off-camera to the right with a serious, resilient expression. Behind him, show a cold North Atlantic harbor with choppy dark water, gray overcast sky, wooden docks, mooring posts, rope, and exactly three visible fishing boats: one large blue-and-red boat in the right background, one turquoise-and-wood boat near the middle right, and one smaller weathered boat partly visible behind his shoulder. Use a Hasselblad medium-format documentary portrait look, shallow depth of field with the fisherman sharply focused and the boats softly blurred, natural cold daylight, muted coastal color palette, fine skin texture, realistic facial pores, cinematic contrast, 1:1 composition, ultra-detailed but natural.
